tool_args.label: Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Key Performance Metrics - January 2026
tool_args.raw_text: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS. January 2026. This performance metrics document was prepared for the January 2026 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees. 2 Broadway • New York, NY 10004 January 26, 2026.

payload.title: Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Key Performance Metrics
payload.prepared_for: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Long Island Rail Road
tool_args.raw_text: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: MTA Metro-North Railroad
tool_args.raw_text: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

tool_args.label: Rob Free, President, Long Island Rail Road
tool_args.raw_text: Rob Free President, Long Island Rail Road

tool_args.raw_text: Justin Vonashek President, Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development, in partnership with the LIRR

tool_args.label: LIRR 2025 Annual Ridership Summary
tool_args.raw_text: Total ridership for the year came in at 81,946,669 million, which is 8.5% higher than 2024. We ended 2025 with a total OTP for the year of 96.17% - our best OTP in recorded history outside of one year during the pandemic.

payload.description: LIRR year-end 2025 ridership and performance results. Total ridership of 81.9 million, 8.5% higher than 2024. 90% of 2019 ridership. Best non-COVID OTP of 96.17%.

tool_args.label: Metro-North 2025 Annual Ridership Summary
tool_args.raw_text: We had healthy ridership growth in 2025, with 71.4 million rides, an annual increase of 6%. For the sixth consecutive year, Metro-North's on-time performance was above 97%, finishing 2025 with an OTP of 97.8%.

payload.description: Metro-North year-end 2025 ridership and performance results. 71.4 million rides, annual increase of 6%. OTP of 97.8% for sixth consecutive year above 97%.

tool_args.label: Queens Interlocking modernization project
tool_args.raw_text: We constantly work to modernize and expand our system, which has led to us to update Queens Interlocking, a major junction point in our system connecting the Hempstead Branch with our Main Line and seeing 333 trains operate through it each weekday. Once complete, the improved switch configuration and modernized signal system will provide enhanced routing options, additional capacity and improve OTP through this vital segment of the Main Line Corridor.

payload.description: Modernizing Queens Interlocking, a major junction point connecting the Hempstead Branch with the Main Line, seeing 333 trains operate through it each weekday. Includes improved switch configuration and modernized signal system to provide enhanced routing options, additional capacity and improve OTP through the Main Line Corridor.

tool_args.label: LIRR ADA Station Improvements Bundle (9 stations)
tool_args.raw_text: This month, MTA Construction & Development, in partnership with the LIRR, celebrated a major milestone in our ongoing commitment to expand station accessibility. Several stations now feature new elevators officially open to the public—including Laurelton, Locust Manor, and Valley Stream. Earlier in October 2025, Auburndale and St. Albans stations also unveiled new elevators. Altogether, nine stations were delivered in a single Design-Build contract.

payload.project_name: LIRR ADA Station Improvements Bundle (9 stations)
payload.description: New elevators and station improvements at multiple LIRR stations. Laurelton, Locust Manor, and Valley Stream stations now feature new elevators. Auburndale and St. Albans stations also unveiled new elevators. Completed stations include Amityville, Copiague, Lindenhurst, and Massapequa Park (completed 2024). Nine stations delivered in a single Design-Build contract. 117 out of 126 LIRR stations are now ADA compliant.

tool_args.label: Harlem Line Station Improvements - Bronx
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development, in partnership with Metro-North, is advancing accessibility and station upgrades at three Harlem Line stations in the Bronx: Williams Bridge, Woodlawn, and Botanical Garden. Inbound platforms are scheduled to reopen in March 2026. The second phase—outbound platform replacements—will run from April to November 2026.

payload.description: Accessibility and station upgrades at three Harlem Line stations in the Bronx: Williams Bridge, Woodlawn, and Botanical Garden. Includes new elevators, platform replacements, stairway upgrades, new canopies. Inbound platforms scheduled to reopen in March 2026. Second phase (outbound platform replacements) from April to November 2026.

tool_args.label: Metro-North Next-Generation Ticket Vending Machines
tool_args.raw_text: We accelerated the rollout of our next-generation Ticket Vending Machines (TVMs), designed to enhance the customer experience. By year-end, 74 of 241 planned machines were in service, with full deployment expected by mid-2026.

payload.description: Rollout of next-generation Ticket Vending Machines (TVMs) designed to enhance the customer experience. By year-end 2025, 74 of 241 planned machines were in service, with full deployment expected by mid-2026.

tool_args.raw_text: In 2025, we implemented our super-express service on the Hudson and New Haven lines. These trains have running times of 90 minutes or less between Grand Central Terminal and the Poughkeepsie and New Haven stations.

tool_args.label: Congestion Relief improvements at Hollis and Forest Hills
tool_args.raw_text: Thanks to Congestion Relief, which celebrated its one-year anniversary this month, both the LIRR Hollis and Forest Hills stations are currently receiving crucial upgrades.

payload.claim_text: Thanks to Congestion Relief, which celebrated its one-year anniversary this month, both the LIRR Hollis and Forest Hills stations are currently receiving crucial upgrades. Hollis is getting comprehensively reconstructed, including brand new, lengthened platforms, a new entrance, and ADA access via new elevators. Meanwhile Forest Hills will see repairs to the historic Tudor station, new ADA-accessible ramps, and lengthened platforms.
payload.subject: LIRR Hollis and Forest Hills stations

tool_args.label: Metro-North Super-Express service implementation
tool_args.raw_text: In 2025, we implemented our super-express service on the Hudson and New Haven lines. These trains have running times of 90 minutes or less between Grand Central Terminal and the Poughkeepsie and New Haven stations.

payload.claim_text: In 2025, we implemented our super-express service on the Hudson and New Haven lines. These trains have running times of 90 minutes or less between Grand Central Terminal and the Poughkeepsie and New Haven stations. We were able to achieve these reduced travel times through a combination of signaling system improvements and strategic routing of these trains. In December, we even added an additional super-express train between GCT and New Haven to meet surging demand for holiday travel to and from the City.
payload.subject: Metro-North Hudson and New Haven lines

tool_args.label: Congestion Relief one-year anniversary
